claim_text: Structural MRI embeddings distinguish AD from cognitively normal subjects.
head: linear probe

site_scanner.detail: scanner predicted as well as outcome (margin -0.05 [95% CI -0.07, -0.04]); likely acquisition artifact

brain_age.detail: brain-age R2=0.24, MAE=4.7yr; effect retained 90% after brain-age-GAP control (Franke/Gaser standard), 85% under the stricter predicted-brain-age control

biomarker_anchor.detail: p-tau217 correlation r=+0.46 (95% CI lower +0.40, n=873)

caveats: p-tau217 measured on a partial subset (~45% missing in a realistic cohort) — anchor is suggestive, not confirmatory.
Verdict is provisional and conditional on the scanner/site distribution of any replication cohort.
Brain-age adjustment uses a proxy control; residual aging signal cannot be excluded.
Any biomarker anchor is complete-case only and may not generalize.
Leakage is bounded, not eliminated — see the published batch-effect audits (‘Batch Effects in Brain Foundation Model Embeddings,’ arXiv:2604.14441 (2026)).

The brain-age control is a proxy, not a gold standard: it is embedding-derived (this cohort's fit: R2=0.24, MAE=4.7yr), so residual generic-aging signal can survive the adjustment and masquerade as disease-specific.
The biomarker anchor likely rests on a partial complete-case subset; with realistic p-tau217 missingness the correlation is estimated on far fewer subjects than the headline cohort.
Confirm the effective sample size per split — subject-disjoint cross-validation on a modest cohort leaves each fold thin.

The leakage test audits the embeddings with the same linear-probe family it uses for the outcome, so it bounds — but cannot fully rule out — shared confounding; this is the mechanic quantified in ‘Pretrained, Frozen, Still Leaking: Auditing Cross-Encoder Attribute Transfer in EEG Foundation Models’ (arXiv:2606.09189 (2026)), which we cite rather than claim.

evidence_ledger.detail: scanner predicted as well as outcome (margin -0.05 [95% CI -0.07, -0.04]); likely acquisition artifact
brain-age R2=0.24, MAE=4.7yr; effect retained 90% after brain-age-GAP control (Franke/Gaser standard), 85% under the stricter predicted-brain-age control
p-tau217 correlation r=+0.46 (95% CI lower +0.40, n=873)
